entry.title: Issue with WRITE statement
entry.content:
Hello, 

I am facing an issue with below WRITE statement, it behaves differently for the 2 different invoices and both invoices belongs to same country and in the logic i did not see any SET COUNTRY statement also. For the 1st invoice date is appearing as 06/05/2025 and for the 2nd invoice date is appearing as 06.05.2025. Please share your suggestions.

write <gs_vbdkr>-fkdat to lv_date.

Transaction: VF02->Repeat Output

entry.title: Re: Global Running Day with SAP Community in Melbourne - Wednesday 4th June 2025
entry.content: Finally had some time to upload my photos from the day! Was not raining thankfully. Was a bit chilly but loved the run.

entry.title: Help Needed: How to Get Last Purchase Price of Product in SAP ByDesign
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

We are currently facing a challenge in SAP Business ByDesign (SAP ByD). Our costing team requires a report that shows the latest purchase price of each product — ideally the last 3 purchase entries, including:

  • Purchase Rate (Net Price)

  • Supplier Code

  • Quantity

Currently, we are able to see full purchase history, but there is no direct way to filter for only the latest entries. For example, if the last purchase was made a year ago, we still want that to show — but we don’t want the full history, only the most recent purchase(s).

entry.title: SAP Signavio: How to Convert yyyy-mm-ddThh:mm:ss (String) to yyyy-mm-dd hh:mm:ss (Timestamp)
entry.content:
Hi,

I am implementing a table from the SAP Signavio process data pipeline, Process Intelligence, SAP Signavio.

I am trying to convert yyyy-mm-ddThh:mm:ss (String) UTC to yyyy-mm-dd hh:mm:ss (Timestamp) JST, but I am unable to resolve the error. Does anyone have any insights?

Data Sample: 2024-11-18T00:33:25 (String), UTC ←"Note that there is a 'T' character between the date and time."
Expected Result: 2024-11-18 00:33:25 (Timestamp), JST

Current SQL Statement:

SELECT
    CASE
        WHEN "OH"."createddate" IS NULL THEN NULL
        WHEN "OH"."createddate" = '[Y]' THEN NULL
        WHEN "OH"."createddate" LIKE '____-__-__T__:__:__%' THEN (TO_TIMESTAMP(REPLACE("OH"."createddate", 'T', ' '), 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MI:SS') AT TIME ZONE 'UTC') AT TIME ZONE 'JST'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "createddate"
FROM "opportunityhistory" AS OH;

Error:
Error: PREVIEW_FAILED
Error ID: 864a2967-c0be-4162-b490-aae51006de7c
Orientation: prod_cloud_os_eu
Error occurred on query preview. Preview execution failed: INVALID_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT: Failed to tokenize string [Y] at offset [0]

#SAP Signavio Process Intelligence

entry.title: IS: Cannot create iFlow in Test tenant. Error while loading the details of existing integration flow
entry.content:
Hi all,

I have setup my test/trial BTP account with Integration Suite / Process runtime. While my setup looks perfectly fine (have doublechecked all the steps), I still cannot create a new Integration Flow inside a package. When I press either 'Add' or 'Add and Open in Editor', nothing happens, no visual feedback (nor an error displayed) and iFlow is not created.

On the other hand, trying to open an existing (copied from pre-existing SAP content) iFlow, displays the following error:

"Error while loading the details of the integration flow. Try opening the artifact again. If the issue persists, report the incident to SAP."

I have checked the existing SAP notes and still cannot find anything meaningful to solve this.


Do you have any ideas ?
Appreciated,
Vil

entry.title: Re: Facing issue in SAP CPI trial version
entry.content: I also have the same issue. I even tried to create a new trial account, but creating artifacts is not possible (here without any error message). On the old trial account every attempt to enter to own artifacts, or in "Discover" section causing error "Error while loading the details of the integration flow. Try opening the artifact again. If the issue persists, report the incident to SAP".

entry.title: Re: Instance creation failed - abap trial
entry.content:
Hi Gaurav,

you had two users in two different of the shared backend systems we use. One in the US region, one in the APJ region.

I deleted both users so that you should be able to try again.

Please make sure that there is no instance left.

See also my following blog post.

How to delete and re-create your ABAP environment ... - SAP Community

Kind regards,

Andre

entry.title: I have a business requirement in SAP EWM
entry.content:
Scenario Recap:

  • 1 Outbound Delivery with 4 Line Items (Products).
  • Each line item is picked from 4 different storage types.
  • The first two products (from one warehouse order) must be picked first and placed in a temporary intermediate storage bin until the second warehouse order is in a locked status.
  • Remaining 2 products are then picked and brought to same intermediate storage bin.
  • Finally, all products are packed into one HU (Handling Unit).
  • HU is then moved to staging area.Could you please guide me on how we can achieve this requirement?
  • SAP Extended Warehouse Management

entry.title: SAP MDK Create Media action failing on IOS platform for the Attachment Control.
entry.content:
Hi,

I'm working with the SAP ODATA V2 API (API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV) to upload attachments in my MDK application. The attachment is getting uploaded on the Android device, but the same operation is failing on an iOS device. I'm using the Attachment Form Cell Control to upload the attachments, and I have created a CreateMedia OData action to handle the upload. I'm getting the below error when I try to upload an image: 

I have also attached the error log details and the Create Media action details. I hope this helps.

Thank you for your help.

Aditya

entry.title: Re: employee central core certification preparation
entry.content:
Good morning,

For any SAP certification you would like to pursue, there is a dedicated learning path available on the SAP Learning Hub. At the end of each course, you’ll find an official exam simulation provided by SAP to help you get familiar with the test format.

If you’re looking for additional practice questions and mock exams, there are also third-party platforms available in the market that offer this type of content.

Here are some useful links to help you get started:

https://learning.sap.com/browse

https://learning.sap.com/courses/sap-successfactors-employee-central-core

I personally took the LMS Certification Exam, but the structure is quite similar across most SAP certifications. Once you complete the SAP training, you’ll gain access to the simulation to help you understand how the real test works.

 

 

When you register for the official certification exam, you’ll have two attempts—so even if you don’t pass the first time, you’ll have another chance to try again.

Let me know if you need any additional support!

Best regards, Juliana Camile 🙂

entry.title: Re: Issue with WRITE statement
entry.content: Is it happening when the user running both the scenarios are same ?

entry.title: Re: Can't instantiate a fragment after Destory it

entry.title: Re: VBAP-FONDS empty (SAP SD and SAP PSM)
entry.content: Unfortunatelly, that note did not help me, but in case somebody has the same issue: FMZUOB ist the correct answer.

entry.title: Re: Vendor Refund for AP Down Payment – Best Practice in...
entry.content:
Hi @nobin_1 ,

You could post a standalone AP Credit Note referencing the original Down Payment (using the Referenced Document option in the Accounting tab). Then, you record an Incoming Payment for a vendor, clearing the Credit you've posted, so the bank and BP would get adjusted.

Finally, when on the DP's relationship map, you'll end up with something like this:

I hope it helps.

BR,
Felipe

entry.title: USEREXIT_SAVE_DOCUMENT_PREPARE and FONDS (SAP PSM)
entry.content:
Dear gurus,

 

we use SAP SD and SAP PSM (public sector management). In VA01 on the account assignment of an item, I can maintain a fund, which is stored in db-table FMZUOB when the SD order is posted.

 

Now I would like to use that data in USEREXIT_SAVE_DOCUMENT_PREPARE when trying to post the order. Unfortunatelly, there are 3.500 public variables available in that exit and I cannot find the itab (?) with the fund-field. VBAP-FONDS is empty...

 

Any ideas?

 

Cheers
Jens

entry.title: VBAP-AUFNR missing in VA01 depending on item category
entry.content:
Dear gurus,

 

we maintain statistical CO orders in account assignments tab in our SD order items. This works fine for all item categories but one (copy of WVN Maint.Contract Item). WVN is an item typ allowing the use of billing plans, which is why we use it.

 

Unfortunatelly, in account assignments tab the field for AUFNR is missing. Can anyone please point out to me why that is and how this can be changed?

 

Cheers
Jens

entry.title: Recruiting Marketing, Radial search without exposing location
entry.content:
A client has the requirement that I am not sure can be met using SuccessFactors Recruiting. They open a couple of stores per week and need to recruit staff. But when they recruit they don't want to share specific information where the store is. What they do on their custom site: candidate enters their home address and the site returns stores in a specific kilometer radius. The candidate cannot see the exact location of the store if it is not yet open.

Is there any way to replicate this with the career site builder? As far as I can see the candidate will always be able to see some location details like city and/or postal code.

Can this type of search be replicated?

entry.title: Duplicated GET requests in odata v4 batch
entry.content:
Hello UI5 experts!

After moving with our apps from 1.208 to 1.120, I started observing strange behaviour in OData V4 batch request to the backend.

I have simple RAP application, using List Report - Object Page floorplan. Object Page is bound to the main entity, and additionally there are a few page fragments with controls bound to different main entity's navigations. When object page renders, OData model sends one big batch containing multiple GET requests - to the main entity and to all sub-entities (from controls added as fragments). So far so good. But for some reason, all GET requests generated from page fragments are duplicated. In general it does not harm anyhow, but it results in some performance issues.

Why is it so? Did anyone observe similar behaviour?

Currently that field is showing all the Business users defined in the system. 

Requirement: client is asking for only limited users list in as Project Mangers. could you please let know where could this be controlled. could this be controlled list in public cloud?

Any help would be really appreciated.

entry.title: Re: Issue with WRITE statement
entry.content: Even though you have mentioned about the SET COUNTRY still can you check the value of TLAND in the NAST Table entries for both of these Outputs ?

entry.title: Re: Recruiting Marketing, Radial search without exposing...
entry.content:
Hi @ArjenVHooydonk ,

Unfortunately, SAP Career Site Builder (CSB) does not natively support this kind of anonymous or fuzzy location search. Here's why:

Job postings on CSB always require a location field (city, state, postal code), and this is typically visible to the candidate.

The map search or radius-based search on CSB filters by proximity, but still shows the location on the job card or job detail page.

There’s no native feature to hide or mask the location field unless it's custom-coded or you use a workaround.

Possible workarounds:

1. Generic Location Naming (Partial solution)
standardize the location names in RMK to generic identifiers:

Instead of “123 King St, Toronto,” use “Toronto - Area A” or “Greater Toronto Region.”

You can map these to internal location IDs for filtering but not expose them directly.

Limitations: City or region still shows; can’t fully hide.

2. Custom Pre-Screen or Job Matching Tool (Best solution)
To replicate the flow you described:

Build a custom job-matching widget or external tool that:

Asks for the candidate's home address.

Performs a geolocation lookup (Google Maps API or similar).

Searches job data stored in a backend (with coordinates).

Returns anonymized roles within X km.

Host this on your own microsite or embed it into CSB as a widget or redirect.

Pros: Full control, hides sensitive info, gives great UX.
Cons: Requires developer effort, coordination with your ATS (SuccessFactors)

The closest option to get to your desired experience in CSB alone is using generic location labels and possibly removing maps or postal codes from the template. For a true geolocation + anonymized location solution, you’ll need a custom frontend layer or pre-screening tool, optionally integrated with CSB or hosted externally.

Hope this helps!! Kindly let me know if you have any queries.

Thanks,

kr,

karthick.

entry.title: Re: Issue with WRITE statement
entry.content: Yes, TLAND value is empty in both cases

entry.title: Re: Suggestion on relevant Certification!

entry.title: Re: Issue with WRITE statement
entry.content: My bad it has got nothing to do with NAST-TLAND since the issue is happening at ABAP Code level itself and not at FORM level. Let's wait for some expert ABAP Developer's comment on this.

entry.title: Re: Do we need additional licences for label printing in...
entry.content:
Hello, 

printing any label or document is part of output management. There is a need of configuration, but after that it should work without any problem. Please see those three articles, I'm sure they will put some light in this issue. 

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/cloud-printing-and-classic-list-printing-via-proxy-printer-in-sap-s-4hana/ba-p/13393764

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/cloud-print-manager-for-pull-integration-installation-and-configuration/ba-p/13334437

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/87f9b54f9c4f4e75aff0061860a6589a/163dd022fde74a48b51bfef9bc97f789.html?locale=en-US

Hope that will help.

Best regards,

Patryk Walędziak

entry.title: I have a question where there is a parameter in one of the job at, but i dont see any value assigned
entry.content:
I am working on validating a job designed by someone else. There is one workflow in which one parameter is being called, i dont understand how it is getting its value. It is showing nothing under the calls section.

The jobs is completing successfully. Does it mean that no value is being passed in the parameter?

entry.title: Re: Data Purging Process in BW4HANA
entry.content:
Hi

i think that your request to "purge" historical data  means to plan a SAP BW Archiving project.

Archiving functionalities depend on your SAP bw version - in the HANA versions you can proceed to analyze  cold store functionalities data tiering.

You can find a lot of technical documentation online regarding SAP BW archiving projects on every version. Deleting physically  years of historical data with Z abap programs ( thousands of requests)  is not a recommended approach and can create deep inconsistencies.

hope it helps

hope it helps

entry.title: Re: I have a business requirement in SAP EWM
entry.content:
Hi @Shivam_D,

I guess this is normal POSC=> Pick---> Pack---> Stage , for Packing station or intermediate bin determination then please do the below settings for intermidiate/pack station determination for same outbound dellivery/consolidation/customer.

"

  • Remaining 2 products are then picked and brought to same intermediate storage bin."

Putaway by Consolidation group: 

  • If you specify the storage type or storage section, the storage type must have the storage type role Work center. If you only specify a storage type, this has to have the putaway rule Consolidation Group . EWM checks these settings when you save your details.

Link for details:

 https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/9832125c23154a179bfa1784cdc9577a/62cccb53ad377114e10000000a174cb4.html?locale=en-US&q=Putaway+by+consolidation+group

Please let us know if you have further questions.

Kind Regards,

Ajit

entry.title: Re: Issue with intent recognition in the Chatbot
entry.content: I observed the same issue as well.

entry.title: Buy holiday
entry.content:
Hi, 

We have a Buy Holiday benefit where employee's can buy holidays every year. We want to tidy the Buy Holiday portlet where employees will have a fresh look as at the moment they have a complete list of previous couple of years buy holidays which they purchased in the few years which is making quite confusing. We want to retain the data like the workflow requests but just clean up the portlet. Is this actually possible just to clean but retain the data please.

Many thanks

Uma

entry.title: Connection to "TMS-QA" established. Response returned: "400: Bad request"
entry.content:
ARCHITECTURE:

firoi app deployed in dev space in one subaccount

WHAT AM I DOING? 

Configuring CTMS for BTP for this fiori app, created another subaccount --> subscribed CTMS there too 

now while creating destination from this newly created subaccount to the dev space of another subaccount where fiori app is deployed -- getting below error while doing connection test

Connection to "TMS-QA" established. Response returned: "400: Bad request"

I have already cross verified credentials and checked content on internet, but it is not helpful

if anyone created this type of destination (from one subaccount to one of the space of another subaccount), pls help

#sapbtp @connectivity #destination #sapdestination #fiori

entry.title: Re: GEF and GIS geo-spatial data to equipment and measure...

entry.title: Re: Issue with Screen Enhancement PBO in SAP AS03 (Asset...
entry.content: We have kept BreakPoint in PBO,unfortunatrly its not caught.

entry.title: Re: Graphical representation of business partner relation...
entry.content:
Hello Sebastian,

In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, the Business Partner (BP) app primarily allows you to view relationships either as:

  • A list view (flat format showing partner relationships),

  • Or a hierarchy (mainly for customer or supplier hierarchies, especially for sales and purchasing orgs).

As of now, there is no native, interactive graphical “tree view” (like an org chart or mind map) of BP relationships in the standard SAP Fiori apps in the Public Cloud. This type of graphical visualization is something you'd more typically find in SAP GUI for S/4HANA On-Premise using tools like transaction BP or third-party solutions.

Best regards,

Patryk Walędziak

entry.title: Re: SAPUI5 Smartfiterbar odata v2 vs macro:filterbar odat...

It's not that feature. What I am looking for is a shortlist of possible values for the field in the filterbar, suggested values or type ahead.

live mode: " the search is triggered automatically when a filter value is changed"

Thanks, but it does not answer my problem.

M.

entry.title: 从入门到上云：您的ERP公有云实施工具箱已更新， 附中文操作指南！
entry.content:
SAP S/4HANA Cloud专家服务团队，自从2019年出道以来，那可是干货满满！我们成功举办了 ‌56场‌ 超实用的“实施基础”‌实战直播课‌，给 SAP全球‌6000多位合作伙伴伙伴们成功“充满了电”。现在，我们要玩点更酷的！‌利用AI视频神器，我们能搞定更多语言需求，服务好咱遍布全球的SAP伙伴大本营啦！

下面是我们“实施基础”课程各主题的视频资源宝库（SAP Media Share），统统备好了 ‌中文版‌，请大家自取：

  • 云思维修炼秘籍 Chinese: Cloud Mindset
  • 创新周期与时间线：别掉队！Chinese: Innovation Cycles & Timelines
  • 探秘“Discover”阶段‌ Chinese: Discover Phase 
  • “Prepare”阶段：万事开头这样顺‌ Chinese: Prepare Phase
  • “Explore”阶段：深度解锁指南‌ Chinese: Explore Phase
  • “Realize”阶段：蓝图变现实 Chinese: Realize Phase
  • “Deploy”阶段：上线冲刺攻略‌ Chinese: Deploy Phase 
  • “Run”阶段：平稳运维之道‌  Chinese: Run Phase 
  • Q-Gate：质量关卡通关秘籍 Chinese: Q-Gate
  • 如何抱紧SAP支持大腿 Chinese: How to Engage with SAP support

翻译中有不当的地方，欢迎大家多多指正！

entry.title: Help with tab & function (F8 & F12) not sending from DS22 to SAP.
entry.content:
I am programming a Zebra DS22 scanner with and ADF (Advanced Data Formatting) program for our receiving process. When tested in excel all programmed commands work properly however when I use the scanner in SAP Functions key (f8 / f12) don't appear to be recognized by SAP. I can also send only one tab command. IE: if I need to press tab on keyboard twice it works but when more than one tab command is sent via scanner it only works once. 

I believe this to be a communication style/type issue but I don't know what type I need or change needs to be made. 

Any insight here that could help get this project across this finish line would be hugely appreciated!

entry.title: Re: NFe Cloud Outbound - Outbound Invoicing Option for Brazil - Como funciona?
entry.content:
Excelente post, Secco!

Uma curiosidade, lendo a documentação do DRC, pra mim não ficou 100% claro é que as BAdIs que utilizavamos no ECC (implementações da CL_PRINT_NFE ou J_1BNF_ADD_DATA, por exemplo) se mantém relevantes para a geração do XML ao enviar para o DRC, assim como é hoje utilizando o GRC (claro que dependente do cliente)?

na leitura das notas (e.g., nota 3091723 sobre CL_PRINT_NFE) me pareceu ser o caso de manter os desenvolvimentos do ECC backend que fazem parte do processo de emissão de notas (com uma resguarda de remediação, caso dê algum problema durante os testes), e a maior mudança é mesmo no "de x para" de BAdIs do GRC para o DRC que ja tém até na documentação Neste Link 

Abraços,

Erick

entry.title: Re: Updating UTR Number in Payment Reference Field After...
entry.content:
Hello Vishnuvikraman,

Yes, it is possible to exchange PAIN.002 messages between the customer and the bank using SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity. However, custom development may be required in S/4HANA to map the <AcctSvcrRef> (the bank’s reference, such as a UTR number) to the corresponding payment document reference field.

Also for your information: related to the tracking information, you may consider if GPI tracking of Swift's SCOREplus service may or may not fit to the requirement; as Swift is evolving SCORE (Standardized Corporate Environment) to SCOREplus, designed for the messages exchanged between corporations and their correspondent banks using XMLv2 ISO 20022 format, it provides the GPI tracking (e.g., Payment Status, Path Info, ETA, Fees, etc.).

Best regards,

Dmitry.

entry.title: Re: unable to see in HTML5 Application in SAP BTP COCKPIT...
entry.content:
When we faced a similar situation, we performed the below steps. 

Then Refresh the html applications. cleared browser cache and cookies and restarted the browser. That did the trick for us. Can you try this or confirm if this exaclty what you have done too?

Julius

entry.title: How to use SAP Joule in Non SAP Applications
entry.content: We are exploring the use of Joule within our non-SAP application. Could anyone please explain the integration process? Specifically, is there an API available that allows us to consume Joule services directly in our application, without relying on SAP Datasphere?

entry.title: node rfc server issue being unreliable
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I set up a node rfc server according to the official documentation. https://github.com/SAP-archive/node-rfc/blob/HEAD/doc/usage.md#nodejs-server-program-example

I set up the ini file, the rfc destination (which successfully passes the connection test), the sec_info, and reg_info.

I'm calling the Node.js RFC function from ABAP using a CALL FUNCTION ... DESTINATION statement.

The issue is sometimes it works and sometimes not. Once it executes the node js function I defined successfully then I restart the server again and then I get the error that the function module (name of the function) not found.

I could not find a reasonable pattern that under what condition it works and under what condition it does not.

I appreciate any help.

I can provide my full codes if needed.

Thanks in advance

@Srdjan

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content: Hello Everyone

entry.title: Universal Allocations- Insurance Company
entry.content:
Hello Community, 

We are an Insurance Company looking to implement Fiori Universal Allocations apps to replace our current custom allocations process. Is there anyone currently working with/for an insurance company that uses these apps and would like to connect? We are looking to discuss how another insurance company uses these apps given the state/national guidelines in insurance reporting. 

Thanks!

entry.title: Certification and Beyond: Stories from SAP’s Top Trainers
entry.content:
Tinus Brink and Lisa Merrill have a lot in common: they both work as SAP consultants and trainers; were both commended as SAP’s highest achieving trainers in the 2024 SAP Authorized Trainer Program; and they’re both passionate about their respective hobbies. But the similarities don’t end there. Their deep-rooted commitment for lifelong learning and inspiring others through education shines through everything they do. And both embrace continuous learning through SAP Learning Hub, balancing their professional expertise with rich personal lives and unique interests.  

From Family Support to Global Impact 

Tinus’ SAP career began thanks to his mother-in-law, who took a leap of faith in him and backed his initial journey with a loan on her home. And her belief in him and in SAP paid off. Today, over 20 of Tinus’ family members and friends now work with SAP in some capacity.  

Originally from South Africa, Tinus has been living in Texas for two years and calls it home. With five SAP Certifications under his belt, ‘Tiny’, as he is affectionately known by those he trains, believes knowledge truly is power: “Being the consultant with the answers not only builds confidence but also strengthens your ability to support clients effectively,” he says. “The skills we gain through SAP training unlock new opportunities for growth and success”.  

Over the years, Tinus has had the privilege of participating in inspiring programs like SAP Skills for Africa, the SAP Young Professional Program, and the Dual Study initiative with various universities.  But his love of learning and training extends outside of the workplace. When he’s not exploring new technologies and experimenting with software and AI, he’s coaching rugby in local schools.  

“Supporting others in their growth and development has always been at the heart of my work”, he explains. “I take great pride in helping new talent enter the SAP ecosystem and kick-start their careers”. And it’s never been easier. The flexibility of remote learning through SAP Learning Hub fits perfectly with his lifestyle: “I can study on weekends or evenings without disrupting family time. I’ve already planned two new certifications this year.” 

Helping Others Dive Deeper Into Their Potential 

Lisa Merrill, an SAP consultant and SAP Authorized Trainer from Chicago agrees. “SAP Learning Hub has been a wonderful tool and there are days when I wonder how we lived without it. I am a better trainer because I have a place that I can go to 24/7 to read, review, and practice.”   

Like Tinus, Lisa’s love of learning opened new doors beyond her career. In 1997, while working on a global SAP implementation, she was introduced to the world of scuba diving, completed a course, and it has been a passion ever since. Today, when Lisa isn’t diving 30-120 feet below sea level in the Bahamas with her family, she’s diving into a new project or training a new intake of SAP users in a classroom.  

As her SAP skills deepened, so did her impact. Over her 25+ year career, Lisa has been fortunate to work on consulting projects as well as provide training to SAP clients around the globe. But a clear highlight is her involvement in SAP’s NS2 Serves initiative that provides military veterans with SAP training, certification, and career opportunities after service. The program provides them with new skills and a fresh start. “It is an honor and a privilege,” she fondly recalls. “Having the opportunity to help veterans prepare for SAP Certifications and later hear about the impact it has made on their lives is incredibly rewarding and fulfilling”. 

Lisa is a firm believer that a lack of training is one of the main reasons projects aren’t successful.  Over the years she has seen companies design great systems that never reach their potential because users aren’t sufficiently trained in the systems. “When I train, I hope, in some small way, that I am contributing to the overall success of an implementation,” she shares. “My motto is: When you stop learning, you stop evolving”.   

A Path to Purpose 

For both Lisa and Tinus, SAP Certification wasn’t just a checkbox. It validated their knowledge, sharpened their skills, and opened meaningful new opportunities to support others on their SAP journeys. With SAP Learning Hub, they’ve been able to stay current, fuel their innate curiosity, and continue doing what they both love most: helping others succeed. 

You too can benefit from self-paced learning opportunities that meet you how you learn best. With SAP Learning Hub, you can unlock guided learning resources including live sessions, practice systems and everything you need to get and stay certified and boost your career.

entry.title: Re: Instance creation failed - abap trial
entry.content:
@Andre_Fischer, Hello. I followed as explained, still I am facing the same issue, date and time also in the attached screenshot.

I tried multiple times closing and reopening browser. Should I proceed again with deleting trial and recreating?

entry.title: Re: SAP MDK Create Media action failing on IOS platform f...
entry.content:
Are you working with an online or offline OData service?

Looking at the CreateMedia action you appear to be passing the attachment content in the Headers as well as defining as the Media.  You should only be specifying it as the Media.   Headers are strings and the error may be due to trying to pass binary data in the header.  Try removing the Headers/Content and see if that fixes it.

entry.title: Offcycle event batch to "termination details".
entry.content: In SuccessFactors we have offcycle event batch for job information and employment details section. Can't that be added to termination details as well? Because to the standard field "Ok To Rehire" I think it should not always be a manual data change. For example in our client side we have a rule saying that a user cannot be "ok to rehire = NO" for more than 20 years. So they suggested us to make make an automation based on a custom date field where they decide when the employee should be ok to be rehired again. It's obviously going to be a future dated field and there is no rules to make it happen. So off cycle event batch could have been a good option if that was feasible. Currently we might have to do it manually.

entry.title: Re: unable to see in HTML5 Application in SAP BTP COCKPIT...
entry.content: done many times still the same

entry.title: Re: Errors in Onboarding 2.0 trying to add or update documents
entry.content: Hi, SAP Support initially found the issue was from a backend configuration toggle that was inadvertently disabled (no idea how this happened). The SAP Engineering team promptly addressed this by enabling the correct setting. Then they needed to validate and reconfigure the PFS user ID and password exactly without any hidden characters or spaces. Then all worked fine again.

entry.title: Re: Next Inspection Date not updated during 301 movement...
entry.content:
Hello there

According to SAP note 3291907,  'Next Inspection Date' is updated:

  1. At the time of Goods Receipt when the field 'Next inspection' is initial for all stock relevant inspection lots (inspection origin 01, 04, 05 and 08).
  2. At the time of Usage decision for recurring inspection lots (origin 09).

In fact, stock transfer postings are specifically excluded in the code:  CHECK dm07m-kzuml IS INITIAL. Which means even an implementation of BAdI MB_BATCH_MASTER method MAINTAIN_INSPECTION_DATE  will not help.

To be perfectly honest,  I don't fully understand why you would want stock transfers to update the 'Next Inspection Date'?

entry.title: Re: Issue with WRITE statement
entry.content:
Date format would initially come from user master record, and a SET COUNTRY will override it. So look for

  • Same user or not, especially if a technical user generates the output in background?
  • There is a SET COUNTRY in form GET_DATA check actual value(s)
*--- Set Country for display conversions e.g. WRITE TO
  set country <gs_vbdkr>-land1.​

entry.title: ISR Number and ISR/QR Reference Fields are missing in FBO3 Vendor line item screen.

entry.content:
Where can I find business process BPMN (best practices) Industry specific process diagrams in SAP for me. Would be helpful, if some one can guide me with screen shots. 

I have looked into Signavio Process Navigator in SAP for me, can able to see only the business process variants but not the BPMN diagrams.

entry.title: Re: VBAP-AUFNR missing in VA01 depending on item category
entry.content:
Normally, if the sale order is integrated with an account assignment objects like

  • an internal order or
  • wbs element or
  • if the sale order is relevant for CO or
  • make-to-stock production with Product Cost by Sales Order

entry.title: Re: Intercompany billing - IDoc segment E1EDP02 021 missi...
entry.content:
Hi Guys, 

   I guess the BADI GV_BADI_SD_INVOICE_IDOC_OUTPUT can be used to add a new entry into the segment E1EDP02 with 021 qualifier (or any other that qualifier for that matter). I will be talking to our development team to activate this BADI so I can test if my assumption is correct.

Good luck,

Marcelo Chamon

entry.title: Re: Filed" scrap "in routing masterdata does not exist in...
entry.content:
Hello,

The Scrap (%) field in routing operations is not included in the standard migration template in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, and cannot be mass-updated after migration.
Your options:

Manual Update
Use the Change Routing app to update Scrap % manually (works for small volumes).

API Update
Use API_PRODUCTION_ROUTING_2 (if available in your tenant) to update scrap via script or integration.

Custom App
Build a small BTP or Fiori app to manage scrap values via API (best for ongoing updates at scale).

Template modification or mass import isn't supported for this field in Public Cloud.

With regards

Chuma

entry.title: Re: SAP MDK Create Media action failing on IOS platform f...
entry.content: Hi Bill, Thanks for replying. The Content header is a mistake in the screenshot as I have removed it and tried this approach before, but still the same error persists. I'm working with a online OData service. Please let me know what further action I can try to make this work.

entry.title: Re: Where can I find business process BPMN diagrams relev...
entry.content:
You will find many of them at Scope Item level, whether for;

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: J58 Accounting and Financial Close https://me.sap.com/processnavigator/SolS/EARL_SolS-013/2502/SolP/J58?region=DE

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition: J58 Accounting and Financial Close https://me.sap.com/processnavigator/SolS/EARL_SolS-055/2023-FPS02/SolP/J58?region=DE

SF EC Employee Central Replication to ERP HCM Payroll: (4AL) https://me.sap.com/processnavigator/SolS/EARL_SolS-016/2405/SolP/4AL?region=DE

As already pointed out, not all industry solutions might have them..

entry.title: Re: How to access and modify the data in QM01 from user-e...
entry.content:
In this enhancement, the FM EXIT_SAPMIWO0_008 should copy the values of I_VIQMEL to the shared area VIQMEL, meaning the Dynpro is already able to display these values.
Be aware that the PAI exit EXIT_SAPMIWO0_009 copies I_VIQMEL to E_VIQMEL before your code copies data from your subscreen to E_VIQMEL.

Have you tried accessing purchase order item data from memory (or the database in case of failure) using an FM such as ME_EKPO_SINGLE_READ (without refreshing or bypassing the buffer), or using a dirty assignment to memory? Have you also checked memory IDs such as MAT and WRK?

entry.title: Re: Overhead Accounting with Universal Parallel Accounting in SAP S/4HANA 2022
entry.content:
Dear Janet,

Thank you in advance for every information you share with everyone and this let us know about this new funcionality deeply.

So, please, let me ask you a question about event-based posting - overhead calculation for internal order with Universal Parallel Accounting. 

According to the Help SAP information: Once you've defined an event-based costing sheet and assigned it to your internal order as described in prerequisites, an overhead calculation is directly carried out for each business transaction. If the posting of a business transaction includes a posting that matches a cost element of the cost basis, an overhead amount is calculated based on the corresponding percentage rate. The result is posted in a separate journal entry with the Actual overhead (realtime) - KZPR business transaction type.

We're testing this and it's working but with some exceptions, for example, we're seing that this option (event-based) only posted in the leading ledger - this means that for a journal entry with just a parallel ledger (journal entry example, posted with "Post general journal entries" app in a parallel ledger and not leading-ledger) the system doesn't calculate any overhead and it doesn't create the separate journal entry with the Actual overhead (realtime) - KZPR business transaction type.

Is it work like this?, is that correct?

I would highly appreciate your advice.

Thank you and best regards,

Estefanía.

entry.title: SAP Process Automation: Simplifying Business Workflow
entry.content:
In today’s fast-paced digital environment, businesses are constantly looking for ways to work smarter and faster. That’s where SAP Process Automation comes in. Whether you're working in finance, HR, procurement, or supply chain, SAP Process Automation can help you get rid of repetitive tasks and streamline your operations. But what exactly is it, and why should you care?

Let’s break it down.

 What is SAP Process Automation?

SAP Process Automation is a solution that allows organizations to automate business processes without writing complex code. It combines the power of workflow management and robotic process automation (RPA) into a single, easy-to-use platform.

Put simply, it helps you:

  • Design business workflows
  • Automate repetitive tasks (like data entry)
  • Reduce manual errors

Free up employees to focus on higher-value work

 Key Features

Here are some of the most useful capabilities of SAP Process Automation:

1. Drag-and-Drop Workflow Builder

Non-technical users can design business processes visually using a simple interface—no coding needed.

2. Bots (RPA)

Software bots can mimic human actions like clicking buttons, filling forms, or copying data from one app to another.

3. Pre-Built Content

SAP offers a library of pre-built workflows and bots for common business scenarios like invoice processing, employee onboarding, or purchase order creation.

4. Integration with SAP and Non-SAP Systems

The tool works seamlessly with SAP S/4HANA and can also interact with external systems like Excel, email, or web portals.

 Real-World Use Cases

Here are a few examples where SAP Process Automation shines:

  •  Finance: Automating vendor invoice approvals and payments
  • HR: Handling employee leave approvals or document generation
  •  Procurement: Automating purchase requisitions and purchase order processing
  • IT: Auto-resolving simple support tickets or resetting passwords

 Benefits of SAP Process Automation

Benefit Description

  • Saves Time Replaces manual, repetitive tasks with automated flows
  •  Reduces Errors Ensures data accuracy and compliance
  •  Cost-Efficient Saves costs by reducing the need for human intervention
  • Scalable Easily adapts as business needs grow
  • Secure Enterprise-grade security and access controls

Getting Started

To begin using SAP Process Automation, you typically need:

  • An SAP BTP (Business Technology Platform) account
  • Access to the SAP Process Automation service
  • Basic understanding of your business process workflows
  • Training or help from SAP documentation or partners

 SAP Process Automation vs Traditional Automation

  • Aspect SAP Process Automation Traditional RPA
  • Integration with SAP Deep integration May need connectors
  • Ease of Use Low-code/no-code Usually requires scripting
  • Intelligence Built-in AI/ML features Limited without add-ons
  • Scalability Cloud-native and scalable Depends on setup

Conclusion

SAP Process Automation is a game-changer for companies running on SAP. It brings together the power of workflow design and robotic automation to simplify complex operations and boost productivity.

If you're tired of chasing approvals, updating spreadsheets, or entering the same data in five different places, it might be time to explore automation.

entry.title: Re: Intercompany billing - IDoc segment E1EDP02 021 missi...
entry.content: Hi Guys, sorry it is me again. I've just checked this BADI and it is restricted to SAP use only. Then, I hope the USEREXIT EXIT_SAPLVEDF_002 could be more appropriate.

entry.title: Re: Novo membro a esse mundo louco chamado SAP
entry.content: Olá! De onde você é, e mexe com o que no SAP?

entry.title: Re: SAP Community Escape House for June – SAP CAP ❄️ 🏔️
entry.content: Glad to be safe and out - staying under a roof in summer heat should count as a survival sport 😄

entry.title: Re: Flexible workflow Supplier invoices for migrated POs
entry.content:
Hello @sanne_larsen 

This issue occurs because the migrated POs lack linkage to PRS, so the flexible workflow defaults to the PO creator instead of the requester. Manually filling in the requester doesn’t work because the workflow logic relies on PR metadata, not display fields.
Best Solution:

Use substitution rules in the “Manage Substitutions for Workflows” app. Assign the correct approver as a substitute for each PO creator so migrated invoices are routed correctly. This is quick to implement and ideal for one-time cleanup.
Optional for Future:

Using key user extensibility and workflow determination logic, you can enhance the workflow condition logic to check a custom “responsible approver” field in the PO when no PR exists.

SAP Help Reference: Manage Substitutions App

With kind regards

Chuma
